payload.title: Unified Engine: dialog-modal-misuse — Only elements that function as dialogs should be tagged as dialog
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `dialog-modal-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse`, `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_success`, `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/dialog-modal-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Marking up elements as dialogs without actual dialog behavior causes screen readers to announce a dialog, misleading users into expecting modal interaction and restricted reading order that do not occur.
**Remediation advice:** Remove role="dialog" from the non-dialog element or add role="presentation" if the HTML <dialog> element is used.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `dialog-modal-misuse` examples published at `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_success` and `/engine/dialog-modal-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

3. Verify rule title, description, WCAG references, and impact (`critical`) are displayed.
4. Confirm navigation breadcrumbs show Engine Rules → dialog-modal-misuse.
steps.expected_result:
Expected Result:
1. Rule detail page loads without errors.
2. Title and description match engine-rules metadata.
3. WCAG references and remediation advice are visible when present.

payload.title: Unified Engine: duplicate-id — The id attribute should have a unique value
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `duplicate-id`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/duplicate-id`, `/engine/duplicate-id_success`, `/engine/duplicate-id_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/duplicate-id/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** The id attribute is a unique identifier in the DOM, and using the same value more than once creates ambiguity. This can cause styles and scripts to behave unpredictably, and may also disrupt label or ARIA references that depend on unique IDs.
**Remediation advice:** Make sure all id attributes on a page have unique values.
**Fixtures:** 2 pass, 1 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `duplicate-id` examples published at `/engine/duplicate-id_success` and `/engine/duplicate-id_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 2 pass, 1 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: emphasis-mismatch — Emphasis should be tagged properly for assistive technology
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `emphasis-mismatch`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/emphasis-mismatch`, `/engine/emphasis-mismatch_success`, `/engine/emphasis-mismatch_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/emphasis-mismatch/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Elements with emphasis importance should have the emphasis role. If not, screen reader users may not understand the emphasis of the text.
**Remediation advice:** Add role=emphasis to the elements that are perceived as emphasis importance.
**Fixtures:** 4 pass, 2 fail

1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `emphasis-mismatch` examples published at `/engine/emphasis-mismatch_success` and `/engine/emphasis-mismatch_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 4 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

payload.title: Unified Engine: figure-discernible — Figure elements should receive text description or lose figure role
payload.description:
**Engine rule ID:** `figure-discernible`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/figure-discernible`, `/engine/figure-discernible_success`, `/engine/figure-discernible_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/figure-discernible/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Figure elements are often incorrectly used to display images on the screen. Incorrectly using the figure tag, without providing a proper figcaption, adds unnecessary clutter to the screen reader user's experience.
**Remediation advice:** Standard images should be coded regularly without having a figure parent element. The figure tag should only be used for images you wish to provide additional visual descriptions for, like the date and time a photo was taken or other metadata. To make a figure tag non-cluttering for screen-readers, add the role=presentation attribute to replace it with another element like DIV. Alternatively, if you did intend to use the figure tag, make sure to add a figcaption tag with the additional metadata
**Fixtures:** 10 pass, 8 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `figure-discernible` examples published at `/engine/figure-discernible_success` and `/engine/figure-discernible_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 10 pass, 8 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A sticky footer remains anchored to the bottom of the screen while the rest of the page content can be scrolled. If it is not offset from interactive elements, it can overlap and obscure the item in focus.
**Remediation advice:** When a page has a sticky footer, make sure that any element receiving focus is not fully covered by the footer. One way to achieve this is by using the CSS property scroll-padding-bottom.
**Fixtures:** 9 pass, 10 fail
payload.preconditions:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `focus-not-obscured-footer` examples published at `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_success` and `/engine/focus-not-obscured-footer_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 9 pass, 10 fail atomic HTML files.
payload.postconditions:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
